meta.confidence_rationale: This plan builds on the existing JSON-based memory usage, minimal dependencies, and proven Git-based workflow for keep/revert. A hybrid template/LLM strategy balances token costs with flexibility.

memory_format.rationale: JSON files are simpler to track in Git, easy to parse, require no additional dependencies, and align with existing skill storage.

task_generation.rationale: Use known template-based improvements for predictable issues, escalate to LLM for novel or complex scenarios where templates do not apply.

keep_revert.rationale: Git-based commits and reverts are auditable, track code changes well, and avoid additional overhead or duplication.

memory_module_location.rationale: Centralizing memory logic in memory.py is cleaner than expanding main.py; we can reuse the existing patterns for vector search and skill storage.

first_test_cycles.rationale: Five cycles allow enough iterations to see multiple keep/revert decisions, ensuring the autonomous loop behaves as intended.

ordered_tasks.name: Persist Episodic Memory to JSON
ordered_tasks.description: Create and maintain an episodic.json file to store experiment outcomes, changes, and scores for each run so they persist across system restarts.
evaluation_criteria: episodic.json is created/updated after each experiment loop
Entries include date/time, changes made, scores, and pass/fail status
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: We must begin by capturing experiment data long-term before we can automate decisions based on past outcomes.

ordered_tasks.name: Implement Git-Based Keep/Revert
ordered_tasks.description: Automatically commit code changes before each improvement and revert if the subsequent evaluation yields a regression.
evaluation_criteria: A new commit is created whenever code is updated
System issues a git revert on negative score deltas
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: After we store experiment metadata, we need a reliable mechanism to keep or discard changes based on performance.

ordered_tasks.name: Implement Template-Based Improvement Logic
ordered_tasks.description: Add a simple decision rule set that proposes improvements based on known shortfalls (e.g., recall below threshold triggers retrieval prompt tweaks).
evaluation_criteria: System suggests a next step if a monitored metric is below a known target
Improvement hypothesis is captured in the episodic.json file
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: Provides a baseline method to propose improvements without burning tokens for every scenario.

ordered_tasks.name: Introduce Heuristic Storage in JSON
ordered_tasks.description: Enable save_skill() to record proven successful strategies in a heuristics.json so they can be retrieved in future runs.
evaluation_criteria: Heuristic entries are saved to heuristics.json upon successful improvements
Heuristics can be retrieved by vector or keyword search
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: We want to retain lessons from prior successes. This complements the template-based logic with historically proven heuristics.

ordered_tasks.name: Wire Memory Updates into Experiment Loop
ordered_tasks.description: Integrate storing experiment results and heuristics into the existing experiment_loop in main.py so that each iteration automatically logs, saves or reverts, and references prior knowledge.
evaluation_criteria: Each loop iteration updates episodic.json and heuristics.json automatically
No manual intervention required for memory updates
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: Completes the pipeline so that each experiment is automatically documented and prior heuristics are consulted for future runs.

ordered_tasks.name: Add LLM-Based Fallback for Novel Improvements
ordered_tasks.description: If the template-based approach finds no matching condition, escalate to the project-tier LLM for a new improvement idea.
evaluation_criteria: System only calls LLM when template logic doesn't match known patterns
Ensures no duplication of token spend on simple, repeated improvements
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: Allows more flexibility after the base logic is established. This step ensures coverage of edge cases but is optional for a minimal loop.

ordered_tasks.name: Run 5-Cycle Automated Test
ordered_tasks.description: Conduct a fully unattended multi-cycle run with the new memory, keep/revert, and improvement logic in place. Observe final net score.
evaluation_criteria: System completes 5 full improvement cycles without human intervention
Git track shows commits for improvements and reverts for regressions
At least one net improvement remains in final code state
ordered_tasks.why_this_order: Verifies the end-to-end flow works in practice. Confirms that the entire pipeline can run autonomously.

minimum_viable_loop.description: The essential loop is: (T-01) record runs, (T-02) keep/revert, (T-03) propose improvements, (T-04) store heuristics, (T-05) wire it up, and (T-07) prove it over multiple cycles. T-06 adds advanced fallback logic but is optional initially.

stop_when: System fails to improve after 3 consecutive changes
Score drops below 0.60 and fails to recover within 2 subsequent attempts
Hardware or cost constraints are exceeded
kill_criteria.pivot_to: Manual review (human operator) for debugging or major architectural changes

verification.how_to_confirm: Perform a fully automated run of 5 cycles, track commits, reverts, and final net improvement in score.
verification.observable_result: An updated codebase and memory files that reflect successful improvements or reverts, with a net positive score gain vs the baseline.
